context: Tests whether the agent applies the correct triage logic from the optimization cycle skill: identifying regressions as highest priority, recognizing that high baselines suggest scenarios may be too easy (consider regenerating), and recommending the right corrective actions before proceeding to tile content edits.

checklist.name: Regression identified
checklist.description: Report identifies the security-review scenario as a regression (with_context 79% < baseline 84%, delta -5)

checklist.name: Regression is highest priority
checklist.description: Report explicitly labels the regression as the highest priority issue — either using the word 'highest priority', 'most urgent', or equivalent strong language that makes it the top item to address

checklist.name: High baseline warning present
checklist.description: Report notes that all three scenarios have high baseline scores (84%, 87%, 82% — all above 80%), suggesting the scenarios may be too easy and agents can solve them without the tile

checklist.name: Scenario regeneration suggested
checklist.description: Report recommends considering regenerating harder scenarios (before or alongside tile content fixes) as a response to the high baseline scores

checklist.name: Tile is actively hurting
checklist.description: Report states or implies that the tile is actively hurting agent performance on security-review (not just failing to help) — because with-tile scores are lower than without-tile scores on that scenario

checklist.name: Per-criterion regression analysis
checklist.description: Report identifies at least two specific criteria within security-review that regressed (e.g., flags_injection_risks dropped from 9→7, checks_auth_bypass 8→6, references_cwe 7→5)

checklist.name: Correct prioritization order
checklist.description: Recommendations are ordered with regression fix BEFORE any suggestion to improve the tile for the other two scenarios — NOT presented as equally weighted items
